title: 10 best herbal creams for high cholesterol

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's wort creams helps with high cholesterol because of its unique composition, rich in hyperforin and hypericin. These bioactive compounds have been shown to improve blood lipid profiles by increasing HDL (good) cholesterol levels, while decreasing LDL (bad) cholesterol concentrations. Additionally, St John's wort cream may help reduce triglycerides and inflammation in the body, further contributing to its potential benefits for cardiovascular health.
remedy_properties: Hyperforin: A bioactive compound in St. John's Wort, hyperforin has been shown to reduce triglycerides and LDL ("bad") cholesterol levels by inhibiting cholesterol absorption in the gut and increasing the excretion of bile acids.
Flavonoids: The flavonoid content in St. John's Wort creams may help improve blood vessel function and reduce inflammation, which can contribute to high cholesterol and cardiovascular disease.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids present in St. John's Wort have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help protect against oxidative stress and cell damage that can contribute to high cholesterol levels.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the St John's Wort plant contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have been shown to help reduce inflammation and improve blood vessel function, making them beneficial for treating high cholesterol.
Seeds: St John's Wort seeds are a rich source of omega-3 fatty acids, which can help lower triglycerides and improve overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_recipe: Gather and dry fresh st john's wort (hypericum perforatum) flowers and leaves in a well-ventilated area for at least three days to reduce moisture content.
Combine 50 grams of dried st john's wort material with 250 milliliters of olive oil in a clean glass jar and store it in the refrigerator for two weeks to infuse.
Strain the infused mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container, squeezing out as much liquid as possible from the solids.
Mix the strained liquid with 200 grams of beeswax, 50 grams of coconut oil, and 20 grams of shea butter in a double boiler until fully melted and well combined.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool to around 60 degrees celsius before whipping it into a smooth consistency with an electric whisk, filling glass jars within an hour for optimal quality.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Littleleaf linden creams helps with high cholesterol because of its unique ability to support cardiovascular health. The herb's flavonoids, including quercetin and epicatechin, help to reduce inflammation in the body, which can contribute to the development of atherosclerosis and high cholesterol. Additionally, littleleaf linden has been shown to improve blood flow and lower blood pressure, further reducing the risk of heart disease. By targeting multiple factors that contribute to high cholesterol, herbal littleleaf linden creams offer a natural and effective approach to maintaining healthy lipid levels.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Littleleaf linden cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to reduce inflammation in the blood vessels, thereby improving blood flow and lowering blood pressure, a major contributor to high cholesterol.

Cardio-protective properties: The herb has been shown to exhibit cardio-protective effects by reducing platelet aggregation, which can contribute to the formation of blood clots that increase the risk of heart disease and high cholesterol.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidant properties of littleleaf linden cream help to neutralize free radicals in the body, which can damage the lining of blood vessels and contribute to the development of atherosclerosis, a condition characterized by plaque buildup in arteries and high cholesterol.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the littleleaf linden plant contain a compound called tilianthine, which has been shown to have hypolipidemic properties, making it effective in reducing cholesterol levels.
Barks: The barks of the plant are rich in flavonoids and saponins, which have been traditionally used to treat cardiovascular diseases, including high cholesterol.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of dried tilia cordata leaves and flowers and grind them into a fine powder.
Combine the powdered tilia cordata with 100 milliliters of sweet almond oil and heat gently for 30 minutes.
Filter the mixture through cheesecloth and discard any solids before transferring it to a clean glass jar.
Add 20 grams of beeswax and heat the mixture for an additional 10 minutes until the wax is fully incorporated.
Allow the cream to cool and solidify at room temperature for at least 30 minutes before transferring it to airtight containers.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hawthorn creams helps with high cholesterol because they contain bioactive compounds that help to reduce LDL ("bad") cholesterol levels while increasing HDL ("good") cholesterol. These compounds, including flavonoids and oligomeric proanthocyanidins, have been shown to improve blood lipid profiles by inhibiting the absorption of dietary fats, reducing triglyceride levels, and enhancing the excretion of cholesterol from the body. As a result, regular use of herbal hawthorn creams may help to alleviate symptoms of high cholesterol, such as hardening of the arteries and heart disease.
remedy_properties: Oligomeric procyanidins (OPCs): OPCs in hawthorn cream have been shown to improve blood lipid profiles by reducing LDL cholesterol and triglycerides, while increasing HDL cholesterol.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in hawthorn cream have antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation in the cardiovascular system, which can contribute to high cholesterol and atherosclerosis (plaque buildup in arteries).

Volatile oils: The volatile oils in hawthorn cream may help improve blood vessel function by relaxing and dilating blood vessels, reducing blood pressure and improving circulation, both of which can contribute to lower cholesterol levels.
remedy_parts: Roots: Hawthorn roots are rich in flavonoids and oligomeric proanthocyanidins, which have been shown to help reduce total cholesterol levels and improve lipid profiles.
Barks: Hawthorn barks contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that may help inhibit the absorption of dietary cholesterol and lower LDL ("bad") cholesterol levels.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried leaves of crataegus monogyna and store them in an airtight container.
Combine 20 grams of dried leaves with 100 milliliters of vodka and let it infuse for two weeks.
Strain the infused mixture through cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle and discard solids.
Mix 30 milliliters of the infused liquid with 10 grams of beeswax and heat in a double boiler until melted.
Pour the cooled mixture into small containers and allow to set for at least an hour before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice creams helps with high cholesterol because they contain bioactive compounds that inhibit the absorption of dietary cholesterol in the gut. The extracts of licorice root, such as glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, have been shown to decrease low-density lipoprotein (LDL) cholesterol levels while increasing high-density lipoprotein (HDL) cholesterol levels, thus improving overall lipid profiles. Additionally, herbal licorice creams may help to reduce inflammation in the bloodstream, which is often linked to increased cholesterol levels and cardiovascular disease risk.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Helps lower triglycerides and LDL (bad) cholesterol levels by inhibiting the activity of enzymes involved in cholesterol production, leading to improved overall lipid profiles.

Flavonoids: Exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress that may contribute to high cholesterol, ultimately improving cardiovascular health.
Isomaltose: Acts as a prebiotic, promoting the growth of beneficial gut bacteria that play a crucial role in regulating cholesterol metabolism and reducing inflammation in the body.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that has been shown to help lower LDL cholesterol levels and improve overall cardiovascular health.
Rhyzomes: Contain bioactive compounds like isoflavonoids, which have been found to inhibit the production of bad cholesterol (LDL) in the liver.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of dried licorice root powder from glycyrrhiza glabra plants and store in an airtight container.
Combine the licorice root powder with 10% beeswax and 20% coconut oil by weight in a double boiler and melt for 30 minutes.
Remove the melted mixture from heat and let it cool to around 120°f to facilitate emulsification with added ingredients.
Add 5% glycerin, 5% vitamin e oil, and 2% lecithin powder by weight to the cooled mixture and stir until fully incorporated for 10 minutes.
Pour the cream mixture into sterilized tubes or containers and allow it to cool completely before refrigerating at a temperature of 40°f for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Milk thistle creams helps with high cholesterol because they contain silymarin, a flavonoid that has been shown to have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Silymarin helps to reduce inflammation in the liver, which can contribute to high levels of LDL (bad) cholesterol in the blood. Additionally, silymarin may help to improve bile flow and increase the elimination of excess cholesterol from the body, leading to improved overall cholesterol profiles. This natural approach can be a valuable adjunct to traditional treatments for high cholesterol.
remedy_properties: Silymarin: Silymarin, a flavonoid compound found in milk thistle, has been shown to help reduce LDL (bad) cholesterol levels by inhibiting the absorption of dietary cholesterol and increasing the excretion of bile acids, which helps remove excess cholesterol from the body.

Silycysteine: Silycysteine, another key compound in milk thistle, has antioxidant properties that can help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation in the liver, which can contribute to high cholesterol levels by promoting the buildup of fatty deposits in blood vessels.

Flavonoids: The flavonoid compounds present in milk thistle cream have been shown to improve lipid profiles by increasing HDL (good) cholesterol levels and reducing triglycerides, which can help remove excess fat from the bloodstream and reduce the risk of cardiovascular disease.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in silymarin, a flavonoid complex that helps reduce LDL cholesterol levels and improve liver function.
Seeds: Abundant in antioxidants and omega-3 fatty acids, which help lower triglycerides and inflammation associated with high cholesterol.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of dried silybum marianum flowers and leaves from your local herb garden or purchase them online.
Grind the dried herbs into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 30 minutes.
Combine the powdered herbs with 8 ounces of coconut oil and 4 ounces of beeswax in a heatproof glass bowl.
Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully melted and the mixture reaches 120°f to 140°f in 15-20 minutes.
Remove from heat, let cool slightly, then whip with a spatula or whisk for 2-3 minutes to create a smooth and creamy consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginseng creams helps with high cholesterol because of its ability to improve blood circulation, increase metabolism, and enhance liver function. The ginsenosides present in these creams help to reduce LDL (bad) cholesterol levels by inhibiting the absorption of dietary cholesterol and increasing the excretion of bile acids from the body. Additionally, ginseng has been shown to activate enzymes that break down fat and reduce triglyceride levels, leading to improved overall lipid profiles and a reduced risk of cardiovascular disease.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Ginseng creams contain antioxidants that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to high cholesterol levels.
Cholesterol-lowering compounds: Some ginseng creams contain compounds like saponins and flavonoids, which have been shown to lower LDL (bad) cholesterol levels and improve overall lipid profiles.

Cardiovascular protection: The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of ginseng creams can help protect against cardiovascular disease by reducing the risk of blood clots, improving blood vessel function, and lowering blood pressure.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins and polyacetylenes, which have been shown to lower LDL cholesterol levels and improve blood lipid profiles.
Rhyzomes: Contain bioactive compounds like ginsenosides, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation associated with high cholesterol.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh panax ginseng roots and clean them thoroughly under cold running water.
Combine 20 grams of dried panax ginseng powder with 100 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond or coconut oil.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler for 30 minutes at 60 degrees celsius stirring occasionally to ensure uniform blending.
Allow the mixture to cool and then strain it through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container.
Store the ginseng cream in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to six months or freeze for longer storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Garlic creams helps with high cholesterol because of its unique ability to lower LDL (bad) cholesterol levels. The herb's active compounds, such as allicin and ajoene, have been shown to inhibit the production of cholesterol in the liver and increase the excretion of bile acids, leading to decreased absorption of dietary cholesterol. Additionally, garlic creams have been found to improve blood vessel function and reduce inflammation, further contributing to its beneficial effects on high cholesterol levels.
remedy_properties: Allicin: The active compound found in garlic, allicin has been shown to inhibit the production of cholesterol in the liver, reducing LDL (bad) cholesterol levels and improving overall lipid profiles.

Volatile sulfur compounds: These compounds, also found in garlic, have been proven to increase HDL (good) cholesterol levels by activating the enzyme responsible for cholesterol metabolism, thereby promoting healthy cholesterol levels.

Antioxidant properties: Herbal garlic creams rich in antioxidants help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which are thought to contribute to high cholesterol development and progression, thus reducing cardiovascular risk.
remedy_parts: Roots: Garlic roots have been traditionally used in folk medicine to help reduce cholesterol levels due to their bioactive compounds and antioxidant properties.
Buds: Garlic buds contain flavonoids and saponins, which may help to inhibit the absorption of dietary cholesterol and lower LDL (bad) cholesterol levels.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh garlic bulbs and dry them completely for at least two weeks in a well-ventilated area.
Grind the dried garlic into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix one teaspoon of the garlic powder with three tablespoons of sweet almond oil and two tablespoons of beeswax pellets in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over medium heat, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully melted and the mixture reaches a temperature of 160°f after 30 minutes.
Remove the mixture from heat, let it cool to room temperature, then strain it through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container for use within six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel creams helps with high cholesterol because of its unique composition. The cream's active ingredients, such as fumaric acid and mucilage, have been shown to reduce triglycerides and LDL (bad) cholesterol levels while increasing HDL (good) cholesterol levels. Fennel's natural anti-inflammatory properties also help to prevent the formation of plaque in arteries, reducing the risk of cardiovascular disease. Additionally, fennel creams may aid in improving blood lipid profiles by enhancing liver function and reducing inflammation in the body.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Fennel cream's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to the development of atherosclerosis (hardening of the arteries) and high cholesterol levels.

Fiber-Rich Content: Fennel cream is rich in dietary fiber, which helps lower LDL (bad) cholesterol levels by binding to bile acids in the gut and removing them from the body, thereby reducing the amount of cholesterol produced in the liver.

Cholesterol-Lowering Compounds: Fennel cream contains compounds like anethole and limonene, which have been shown to inhibit the production of cholesterol in the liver and reduce LDL cholesterol levels by improving blood lipid profiles.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in fiber and antioxidants, making it effective in reducing LDL cholesterol levels.
Seeds: Containing essential oils like anethole, which has been shown to improve lipid profiles and lower triglycerides.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of fresh foeniculum vulgare leaves and stems then dry them using a food dehydrator for 4 hours at 95 degrees fahrenheit.
Blend 1 cup of dried foeniculum vulgare with 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 4 ounces of sweet almond oil in a slow cooker on low heat for 3 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bowl then discard the solids and allow the liquid to cool to room temperature.
Whip the cooled liquid using an electric mixer until it thickens to a creamy consistency adding 2 drops of lemon essential oil as preservative.
Pour the whipped cream into sterilized glass jars leaving 1/4 inch headspace and store them in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ceylon cinnamon creams helps with high cholesterol because they contain a unique combination of bioactive compounds, including cinnamaldehyde and coumarin, which have been shown to have potent lipid-lowering properties. These compounds help to increase the body's production of LDL receptors, allowing for more efficient removal of low-density lipoprotein (bad) cholesterol from the bloodstream. Additionally, Ceylon cinnamon has been found to reduce triglycerides and increase high-density lipoprotein (good) cholesterol levels, leading to improved overall cardiovascular health and reduced risk of chronic diseases.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: The cream's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce inflammation in the body, which can contribute to high cholesterol levels and cardiovascular disease.
Alpha-hydroxy acid: Alpha-hydroxy acid present in Ceylon cinnamon helps break down triglycerides and LDL (bad) cholesterol, making it easier for the body to excrete them.
Polysaccharides: The polysaccharides in herbal Ceylon cinnamon creams have been shown to reduce blood lipid levels and improve liver function, both of which can help lower high cholesterol.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, rhyzomes are used to reduce inflammation and improve lipid profiles.
Barks: Containing cinnamaldehyde, barks help to inhibit cholesterol production and lower LDL (bad) cholesterol levels.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried cinnamomum verum bark and grind it into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 2 minutes.
Combine the powdered cinnamomum verum bark with 250ml of distilled water in a heat-resistant glass bowl, then simmer it over low heat for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container, discarding the solids and reserving the liquid extract.
Mix 100g of beeswax with 200ml of coconut oil in a double boiler, heating them until melted and fully incorporated for 10 minutes.

Combine the cinnamomum verum liquid extract with the melted beeswax-coconut oil mixture, stirring well to create a smooth and even cream consistency, allowing it to cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chebulic myrobalan creams helps with high cholesterol because they contain a unique combination of antioxidants, flavonoids, and polyphenols that work together to lower LDL (bad) cholesterol levels. The herbs in the cream have been shown to inhibit the absorption of dietary cholesterol, reduce triglycerides, and improve blood lipid profiles. Additionally, chebulic myrobalan has anti-inflammatory properties that help to protect against arterial plaque buildup, further reducing the risk of cardiovascular disease associated with high cholesterol.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Chebulic Myrobalan contains potent antioxidants that help neutralize free radicals in the bloodstream, which can contribute to the development and progression of atherosclerosis (plaque buildup) in arteries, reducing the risk of heart disease associated with high cholesterol.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in Chebulic Myrobalan cream may help reduce inflammation in blood vessels, which is often exacerbated by high levels of LDL ("bad") cholesterol, thus promoting a healthier cardiovascular environment.

Hypolipidemic effects: Chebulic Myrobalan has been traditionally used to lower lipid levels (e.g., triglycerides and LDL) in the bloodstream, which can help manage high cholesterol and reduce the risk of cardiovascular complications.
remedy_parts: Fruits: The fruits of the chebulic myrobalan plant contain a significant amount of tannins and flavonoids, which are known to have antioxidant properties that help reduce LDL (bad) cholesterol levels.
Barks: The barks of the chebulic myrobalan plant contain gallic acid, which has been shown to inhibit the production of cholesterol in the liver, thus reducing overall cholesterol levels.
remedy_recipe: Collect dried terminalia chebula powder weighing 20 grams from reputable suppliers.
Mix the terminalia chebula powder with 100 milliliters of cold-pressed coconut oil in a heat-proof bowl.
Heat the mixture under low heat for 10 minutes stirring occasionally to facilitate infusion.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into another container and discard the solids after 30 minutes.
Allow the cream to cool and solidify at room temperature before transferring it to glass jars for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Guggul creams helps with high cholesterol because they contain Commiphora mukul, a natural herb that has been used for centuries to lower LDL (bad) cholesterol levels. The active compounds in guggul, such as gum resin and essential oils, have been shown to inhibit the production of bile acids, which in turn reduces the absorption of dietary cholesterol into the bloodstream. This helps to regulate cholesterol levels and improve overall cardiovascular health by reducing plaque buildup and inflammation in the arteries. Regular use of guggul creams may also help to increase HDL (good) cholesterol levels, further supporting heart health.
remedy_properties: Guggulsterones: These phytochemicals have been shown to significantly lower LDL (bad) cholesterol levels and triglycerides, while also increasing HDL (good) cholesterol levels by inhibiting the enzyme responsible for cholesterol production in the liver.
Antioxidant properties: Guggul creams contain potent antioxidants that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to high cholesterol and cardiovascular disease.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in guggul creams may help reduce inflammation in the blood vessels, which is a known risk factor for high cholesterol, heart disease, and stroke.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in a resin called gum guggul, which has been shown to reduce LDL cholesterol levels and improve triglyceride profiles.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes contain a compound called boswellic acid, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms of high cholesterol, such as joint pain.
remedy_recipe: Grind 2 tablespoons of dried commiphora mukul resin into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 3 minutes.
Combine the ground commiphora mukul powder with 4 ounces of coconut oil in a double boiler on low heat for 15 minutes.
Add 1 tablespoon of beeswax and stir until fully melted and dissolved, taking approximately 5 minutes.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool to room temperature for 10 minutes before whipping into a creamy consistency with an electric mixer.
Pour the final cream into sterilized jars and store in the refrigerator at a temperature of 40°f to 50°f for up to 2 weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dog holly creams help with high cholesterol because they contain potent herbs that naturally lower triglyceride levels and reduce LDL (bad) cholesterol. The cream's active ingredients, such as berberine and turmeric, have been shown to improve blood lipid profiles by inhibiting the production of cholesterol in the liver and increasing the excretion of bile acids from the body. This leads to a reduction in overall cholesterol levels, helping to alleviate symptoms associated with high cholesterol, including hardening of the arteries and increased risk of heart disease.
remedy_properties: Flavonoids: Flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, have been shown to reduce LDL (bad) cholesterol levels by inhibiting cholesterol absorption in the gut and increasing the excretion of bile acids, leading to a decrease in liver cholesterol production.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids, including caffeic acid and ferulic acid, exhibit antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation in blood vessels and improve blood lipid profiles by lowering LDL cholesterol levels and increasing HDL (good) cholesterol levels.

Saponins: Saponins, such as glycosides and triterpenoids, have been found to have a hypocholesterolemic effect by inhibiting the absorption of dietary cholesterol and reducing bile acid reabsorption in the gut, leading to increased elimination of cholesterol from the body.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and saponins, which have been shown to help lower LDL cholesterol levels and improve lipid profiles.
Rhyzomes: Contain bioactive compounds like sesquiterpenes and triterpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can aid in reducing cholesterol buildup.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and clean ruscus aculeatus leaves by washing them thoroughly in warm water and then drying them immediately.
Dry the cleaned ruscus aculeatus leaves using an oven set at a low temperature of 50-60°c for 2 hours.
Grind the dried ruscus aculeatus leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix the ground ruscus aculeatus powder with coconut oil and beeswax in a 1:1 ratio at room temperature for 10 minutes, until well combined.
Heat the mixture gently in a double boiler over low heat for 15-20 minutes or until it reaches a temperature of 70°c to create a smooth and creamy texture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chaste tree creams helps with high cholesterol because they contain flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoid saponins, which work together to reduce LDL (bad) cholesterol levels and increase HDL (good) cholesterol levels. The cream's active compounds also have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help lower triglycerides and prevent the formation of plaque in arteries. Additionally, chaste tree creams may help regulate blood sugar levels and improve overall cardiovascular health, making them a natural and effective adjunct therapy for managing high cholesterol.
remedy_properties: Pseudopelletierine: This compound has been shown to inhibit the activity of HMG-CoA reductase, an enzyme involved in cholesterol synthesis, thus reducing cholesterol production and levels.
Iridoid glycosides: These compounds have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce inflammation in blood vessels and improve overall cardiovascular health.
Steroids: The steroids present in chaste tree cream have been shown to have a hypolipidemic effect, meaning they can help lower triglyceride levels and improve overall lipid profiles.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and terpenes, the roots of the chaste tree have been traditionally used to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health, making them suitable for high cholesterol creams.

Fruits: The fruits of the chaste tree contain a variety of bioactive compounds that have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in reducing lipid peroxidation and improving overall heart health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh vitex agnus castus leaves and stems in late summer and dry them thoroughly within 24 hours using desiccation or shade drying methods.
Combine 20 grams of dried vitex agnus castus leaves and stems with 100 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days to allow for maceration and infusion of the herbal extract into the carrier oil.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter to separate the infused oil from the solids, then transfer it to a clean container.
Allow the infused oil to mature for an additional 7 days before whipping it with 20 grams of beeswax and 10 milliliters of vitamin e oil to create a smooth and consistent cream texture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger creams helps with high cholesterol because they contain bioactive compounds that help to improve blood flow, reduce inflammation, and lower triglyceride levels. The anti-inflammatory properties of ginger also help to relax blood vessels, making it easier for cholesterol-laden plaques to dissolve and clear from the bloodstream. Additionally, ginger's ability to stimulate digestion and absorption helps to remove excess cholesterol from the body, leading to improved overall cardiovascular health and reduced risk of heart disease.
remedy_properties: Anti-Inflammatory compounds: Ginger contains anti-inflammatory compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which can help reduce inflammation in the blood vessels and improve lipid profiles by decreasing triglycerides and LDL cholesterol.

Vasodilatory properties: Ginger's vasodilatory effects can help widen the arteries, improving blood flow and reducing resistance to blood flow, which can lead to decreased blood pressure and improved circulation, both of which can contribute to reduced cholesterol levels.

Cholesterol-lowering flavonoids: Herbal ginger creams often contain flavonoids like hesperidin and naringenin, which have been shown to inhibit the absorption of dietary cholesterol and increase bile secretion, leading to increased excretion of excess cholesterol from the body.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which have been shown to reduce LDL cholesterol levels and improve cardiovascular health.
Rhyzomes: Contains a potent mixture of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help to lower triglycerides and LDL cholesterol while increasing HDL cholesterol.
Barks: High in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which can help to reduce inflammation and improve blood lipid profiles by inhibiting the production of cholesterol.
remedy_recipe: Harvest zingiber officinale rhizomes in the morning after dew has evaporated for optimal quality.
Dry the harvested rhizomes at 60 degrees fahrenheit for 7-10 days to remove excess moisture.
Grind 30 grams of dried zingiber officinale into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 20 milliliters of sweet almond oil with 10 milliliters of beeswax and heat the mixture at 160 degrees fahrenheit for 30 minutes to create a base cream.
Combine the zingiber officinale powder with the base cream, stirring well to ensure thorough incorporation, and store in an airtight container for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Oats creams helps with high cholesterol because of their unique ability to lower LDL (bad) cholesterol levels. The beta-glucans present in oats act as soluble fibers that bind to bile acids in the digestive tract, preventing them from being reabsorbed into the bloodstream and ultimately reducing the amount of cholesterol produced in the liver. This natural process helps to reduce overall cholesterol levels, improving cardiovascular health and decreasing the risk of heart disease.
remedy_properties: Beta-Glucans: These soluble fibers from oat kernels help lower LDL (bad) cholesterol levels by binding to bile acids and removing them from the body, thereby reducing the amount of cholesterol produced in the liver.

Saponins: The saponins present in herbal oat creams have been shown to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to high cholesterol, and also help to lower triglycerides and LDL cholesterol levels.

Fibers and Avenanthramides: The fiber content of herbal oat creams helps to slow down the absorption of dietary fats and cholesterol into the bloodstream, while avenanthramides, a type of polyphenol, have been found to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory compounds that can contribute to high cholesterol.
remedy_parts: Roots: Oat roots have been traditionally used to lower cholesterol levels and reduce triglycerides in the blood, making them an effective ingredient in medicinal creams.
Seeds: Oat seeds contain a type of fiber called beta-glucan, which has been shown to help remove excess cholesterol from the digestive system and prevent its re-absorption into the bloodstream.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh avena sativa leaves and stems, drying them thoroughly within 24 hours of collection.
Combine 20g of dried avena sativa herb with 50g of coconut oil and 10g of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture for 30 minutes at 60°c, stirring occasionally, to infuse the oil with the herbal extract.
Strain the hot mixture through cheesecloth into a clean container, discarding the solids and allowing it to cool.
Whip the cooled mixture with an electric mixer until smooth and creamy, packaging the finished cream within 72 hours of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric creams helps with high cholesterol because they contain curcumin, a potent compound that has been shown to lower LDL (bad) cholesterol levels. Curcumin works by inhibiting the production of enzymes in the liver that promote cholesterol synthesis, thereby reducing the amount of LDL cholesterol produced. Additionally, turmeric creams have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the bloodstream, which can contribute to high cholesterol. Regular use of turmeric creams may help improve overall cardiovascular health and reduce the risk of heart disease.
remedy_properties: Curcumin: Turmeric's active compound, curcumin, has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce triglyceride levels and improve lipid profiles by inhibiting the activity of enzymes involved in cholesterol synthesis.

Flavonoids: Turmeric creams contain flavonoids like quercetin, which have been shown to have cholesterol-lowering effects by increasing bile acid production and reducing the absorption of dietary cholesterol.

Terpenes: Terpenes present in turmeric cream, such as limonene and pinene, have been found to inhibit the activity of HMG-CoA reductase, the enzyme responsible for converting amino acids into cholesterol, thus lowering LDL (bad) cholesterol levels.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzome of turmeric contains a high concentration of curcuminoids, which have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it effective in reducing cholesterol levels.
Barks: Turmeric barks contain compounds like kaempferol and quercetin that help to reduce triglycerides and LDL (bad) cholesterol levels, while also improving HDL (good) cholesterol.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh turmeric roots, rinsing them thoroughly under cold running water and pat dry with paper towels within 24 hours of harvest.
Dry the turmeric roots in a single layer at room temperature for 7-10 days or until they become brittle and fragrant weighing about 100g.
Grind dried turmeric roots into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for approximately 30 seconds or until smooth adding 20ml of cold water as needed.
Mix the ground turmeric powder with 50g of coconut oil and 10g of beeswax in a double boiler setup heating mixture over low heat for 15-20 minutes stirring occasionally.
Remove from heat and let cool to around 50°c before whipping the mixture with an electric mixer or whisk until smooth and creamy for about 2-3 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory activity: Kansas coneflower contains flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce inflammation in blood vessels, thereby contributing to improved lipid profiles and reduced risk of cardiovascular disease.

Antioxidant activity: The cream's antioxidant compounds, such as kaempferol and quercetin, can neutralize free radicals and protect against oxidative stress, which may help prevent the oxidation of LDL cholesterol and reduce its accumulation in arterial walls.

Cardiovascular-protective terpenoids: Kansas coneflower contains triterpene saponins, specifically ursolic acid and oleanolic acid, which have been shown to exhibit cardiovascular-protective effects by reducing triglycerides, LDL cholesterol, and blood pressure, while also improving HDL cholesterol levels.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which can help to reduce inflammation and improve lipid profiles.
Barks: The barks contain terpenoids, which have been shown to inhibit cholesterol absorption and reduce low-density lipoprotein (LDL) levels.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh echinacea angustifolia roots in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Dry the roots for at least 24 hours using air circulation or a food dehydrator on low heat.
Grind dried echinacea angustifolia roots into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine 10 grams of ground echinacea angustifolia with 20 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler at medium heat for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean container and store in an airtight jar in a cool dark place.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower creams helps with high cholesterol because they contain a unique blend of natural ingredients that have been shown to effectively lower LDL (bad) cholesterol levels. The herb, Echinacea angustifolia, has been traditionally used to promote cardiovascular health and reduce inflammation in the body, which can contribute to high cholesterol. Additionally, the cream's fatty acid-rich formula helps to dissolve and remove excess cholesterol from the bloodstream, supporting overall heart health and well-being.

Rosemary creams helps with high cholesterol because of its unique ability to stimulate blood flow and improve circulation. The antioxidants present in rosemary help to reduce inflammation, which is a common contributor to high cholesterol. Additionally, rosemary has been shown to have lipid-lowering properties, helping to lower triglyceride levels and increase HDL (good) cholesterol. By incorporating rosemary creams into your daily routine, you may experience improved cardiovascular health, reduced risk of heart disease, and overall enhanced well-being.
remedy_properties: Rosemary's carnosic acid: Carnosic acid, a polyphenol found in rosemary, has been shown to reduce LDL ("bad") cholesterol levels by inhibiting the absorption of dietary cholesterol and increasing the excretion of bile acids.

Rosemary's anti-inflammatory compounds: Rosemary contains various anti-inflammatory compounds like rosmarinic acid and camphor, which help reduce inflammation in blood vessels and improve blood lipid profiles, thereby aiding in cholesterol management.

Rosemary's antioxidant activity: Rosemary's antioxidants, including vitamin E and beta-carotene, neutralize free radicals that can damage blood vessel walls and contribute to high cholesterol, thus helping to protect against cardiovascular disease.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rosemary roots are used due to their rich antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce inflammation associated with high cholesterol.
Barks: The barks of the rosemary plant are used for their ability to stimulate blood circulation and improve lipid profiles, thereby helping to lower cholesterol levels.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves and dry them for 2 weeks in a dark place.
Combine 100ml of sweet almond oil and 20g of dried rosemary leaves in a clean glass jar.
Infuse the mixture in a warm place for 4 hours or overnight, then strain it through cheesecloth into another container.
Mix 30g of beeswax and 70g of coconut oil with the infused herbal liquid, stirring until melted and smooth.
Pour the cream mixture into sterilized glass jars and seal them tightly, storing in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mallow creams helps with high cholesterol because they contain mucilages, a type of fiber that can bind to bile acids in the digestive tract, reducing their absorption back into the bloodstream. This leads to increased elimination of cholesterol from the body, thereby helping to lower LDL ("bad") cholesterol levels and improve overall cardiovascular health. Additionally, mallow creams may also help reduce triglycerides and increase HDL ("good") cholesterol, further supporting healthy cholesterol profiles.
remedy_properties: Beta-Glucans: Mallow contains beta-glucans, which are types of soluble fiber that can help lower cholesterol levels by binding to bile acids and removing them from the body, reducing the amount of cholesterol produced in the liver.
Saponins: Mallow's saponin content has been shown to inhibit the absorption of dietary cholesterol and triglycerides, leading to a reduction in blood lipid levels and improved cardiovascular health.

Anti-Inflammatory Compounds: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in mallow, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, can help reduce inflammation in the body, which is a known risk factor for high cholesterol and cardiovascular disease.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in mucilages, which help to reduce inflammation and improve lipid profiles, making them effective in reducing high cholesterol.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes contain saponins, which have been shown to lower LDL (bad) cholesterol levels by inhibiting the absorption of dietary cholesterol.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried malva sylvestris flowers in quantities of 50 grams for use in this recipe.
Mix 20 grams of dried malva sylvestris flowers with 100 milliliters of hot distilled water and steep for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean container and discard the solids.
Combine the herbal infusion with 50 grams of beeswax, 20 grams of coconut oil, and 10 grams of shea butter in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until it reaches a smooth, creamy consistency suitable for use as a topical cream.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for high cholesterol are topical preparations made from natural ingredients, such as herbs, spices, and essential oils, designed to help manage and reduce cholesterol levels. These creams work by improving blood lipid profiles, reducing inflammation, and increasing circulation, ultimately lowering the risk of heart disease and stroke. Examples include creams containing saw palmetto, guggul, and omega-3 fatty acids, which have been shown to improve triglyceride levels and LDL (bad) cholesterol. By using these creams, individuals can experience improved overall health, reduced risk of cardiovascular events, and enhanced quality of life.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat high cholesterol with herbal creams is by using formulations that combine potent herbs like berberine-rich goldenseal, plant sterols and stanols, and beta-sitosterol. These natural ingredients help lower LDL ("bad") cholesterol levels, while increasing HDL ("good") cholesterol levels. Additionally, look for creams containing omega-3 rich flaxseed or evening primrose oil to further support heart health. Regular application and consistent use can lead to effective results.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to high cholesterol that are treated with herbal creams are conditions such as eczema, psoriasis, and dermatitis. Herbal creams containing ingredients like aloe vera, chamomile, and tea tree oil can help reduce inflammation and soothe skin irritation, promoting healthy circulation and skin regeneration. These natural remedies may also aid in reducing itching and scaling associated with these conditions.
